steps.title: Identify what the examples show about the artists
steps.content: The examples show artists deliberately blending Indigenous musical elements with other styles: throat singing mixed with electric music, powwow melodies fused with rock. The artists are treating Indigenous and non-Indigenous styles as combinable rather than as separate categories. The blank must describe this attitude toward the boundary between them.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'disguise': Disguise means to conceal. If artists disguised the distinctions, the Indigenous elements would be hidden — but both examples keep those elements prominent and recognizable. ✗
  • Choice B — 'reject': Reject means to refuse to accept. "Artists reject distinctions between Indigenous music and other styles" means they refuse to treat the two as separate categories — exactly what blending them demonstrates. ✓
  • Choice C — 'replace': Replace means to substitute one thing for another. The artists are not removing Indigenous music and putting something else in; they are combining styles. ✗
  • Choice D — 'observe': Observe means to notice or keep. Observing distinctions would mean honoring the separation between styles — the opposite of what the examples show. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
